title: National Pension System (NPS)
meta_description: NPS provides market-linked retirement savings with tax benefits under 80CCD. Check returns, tax benefits, withdrawal rules, and how to open NPS account.

hero.one_liner: Market-linked retirement savings scheme with additional ₹50,000 tax deduction under Section 80CCD(1B) — beyond the ₹1.5L 80C limit

what_is_it: National Pension System (NPS) is a voluntary, long-term retirement savings scheme regulated by PFRDA (Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority). Unlike PPF or FD, NPS invests your money in a mix of equity, government bonds, and corporate debt — giving potentially higher returns.

NPS has two types of accounts: Tier I (retirement account with tax benefits, restricted withdrawal) and Tier II (savings account, flexible withdrawal, no tax benefit). The biggest draw is the additional ₹50,000 tax deduction under Section 80CCD(1B), which is over and above the ₹1.5 lakh limit of Section 80C. At retirement (60), you must use at least 40% of the corpus to buy an annuity (monthly pension), and can withdraw up to 60% as lump sum (tax-free).

eligibility.label: Account types
eligibility.value: Tier I (retirement, tax benefits) + Tier II (flexible, no lock-in)

eligibility.label: Documents
eligibility.value: PAN card, Aadhaar, bank account, passport photo

how_to_apply.title: Visit enps.nsdl.com (eNPS portal)
how_to_apply.description: Click 'Registration' → New Registration → select 'All Citizens of India'.

how_to_apply.title: Complete eKYC
how_to_apply.description: Verify using Aadhaar OTP or PAN + bank account verification. Fill personal details and nominee info.

how_to_apply.title: Choose investment option
how_to_apply.description: Select Active Choice (you pick equity/debt %) or Auto Choice (system manages based on your age). Choose a Pension Fund Manager (SBI, HDFC, ICICI, etc.).

how_to_apply.title: Make first contribution and get PRAN
how_to_apply.description: Pay minimum ₹500 online. You receive a PRAN (Permanent Retirement Account Number). Use this to manage your NPS account.

important_alert.text: NPS is best for the extra ₹50,000 tax deduction under 80CCD(1B). If you've already maxed out your 80C limit of ₹1.5 lakh, NPS gives you an additional ₹50K deduction — saving up to ₹15,600 in tax (at 30% slab).

important_dates.label: Tax benefit deadline
important_dates.value: Invest before March 31 for current FY deduction

faqs.question: What returns does NPS give?
faqs.answer: Returns depend on your asset allocation. Historically, equity option has given 10-14% and corporate bonds 8-10% over 10-year periods. This is not guaranteed — NPS is market-linked.

faqs.question: Can I withdraw before retirement?
faqs.answer: After 3 years, you can withdraw up to 25% of your own contributions for specific reasons: children's education, marriage, home purchase, or medical treatment. Maximum 3 partial withdrawals allowed.

faqs.question: NPS vs PPF — which is better?
faqs.answer: NPS gives higher potential returns (market-linked) and extra ₹50K tax benefit. PPF gives guaranteed 7.1% and full tax-free maturity. Ideally, do both: PPF for guaranteed safe returns, NPS for the extra tax benefit and higher growth potential.

faqs.question: What happens to NPS if I die?
faqs.answer: The entire accumulated corpus is paid to the nominee. No mandatory annuity purchase needed. The amount is tax-free for the nominee.
